cream-paper.$description: Cream Paper — Page canvas, card surfaces, inverted text on dark fills

botanical-ink.$description: Botanical Ink — Primary headings, body text, outlined/ghost button borders, nav text

bark-brown.$description: Bark Brown — Decorative fill, secondary text tone, SVG illustration depth

warm-loam.$description: Warm Loam — Primary action button fill — the single chromatic CTA, deep earthy brown against cream

forest-floor.$description: Forest Floor — Green outline accent for tags, dividers, and focused UI edges. Do not promote it to the primary CTA color

sage-mist.$description: Sage Mist — First elevated surface above canvas — subtle section banding

lichen.$description: Lichen — Secondary surface tint, pill button borders, hairline dividers

moss-veil.$description: Moss Veil — Tag/badge backgrounds, soft highlight washes, selected state fills

eucalyptus.$description: Eucalyptus — Tertiary surface, large decorative borders, muted section frames

onyx.$description: Onyx — Logo mark fill, SVG icon strokes, maximum-contrast elements

akkurat.$description: Primary typeface across all UI — nav, body, headings, buttons. The tight -0.04em tracking at display sizes (47-53px) pulls letters together, giving headlines a dense, inked-on-page quality. Weight 400 is default; 700 reserved for emphasis and nav items. Akkurat's humanist grotesque proportions soften the technical context, making the AI/agent product feel editorial rather than engineering.

fragment-mono.$description: Micro-labels, status indicators, tag text, code-adjacent annotations. Used sparingly as typographic punctuation — never for running text. The slight +0.02em tracking widens the mono for legibility at small sizes. Functions as the system's 'technical whisper'.

canvas.$description: Surface level 0: Page background — warm off-white with green undertone

first-elevation.$description: Surface level 1: Subtle section banding, alternating content blocks

second-elevation.$description: Surface level 2: Card surfaces, pill button outlines, bordered containers

third-elevation.$description: Surface level 3: Active states, selected highlights, accent tags

fourth-elevation.$description: Surface level 4: Decorative frames, large section borders, muted overlays
